practices.name: Front Range Recruiting

evidence.label: No HTTPS / insecure
evidence.evidence_str: The site does not load over a valid HTTPS connection, so browsers flag it as “Not secure” in the address bar.

evidence.label: No mobile viewport tag
evidence.evidence_str: The homepage has no mobile viewport tag, so it isn’t built to adapt to phone screens — mobile visitors get a zoomed-out desktop layout.

evidence.label: No clear call-to-action
evidence.evidence_str: The homepage has no clickable phone number, booking link, contact form, or email visible in the page source.

practices.draft_observation: Front Range Recruiting's site doesn’t load over a secure https connection, so browsers show visitors a “Not secure” warning.

practices.name: Cedar Park Family Counseling

evidence.label: No website at all
evidence.evidence_str: Cedar Park Family Counseling has no website at all — anyone searching for them online finds nothing.

practices.draft_observation: I went looking for Cedar Park Family Counseling's website and couldn’t find one — anyone searching for you online comes up empty.

practices.name: Summit Consulting Group

evidence.label: Very thin homepage
evidence.evidence_str: The homepage has only ~21 words of content and 1 links — barely more than a placeholder.

evidence.label: No clear call-to-action
evidence.evidence_str: The homepage has no clickable phone number, booking link, contact form, or email visible in the page source.

practices.draft_observation: Summit Consulting Group's homepage is pretty sparse — there’s very little there for someone deciding whether to reach out.
